stemHtml: A captain who has just recently been trained to a new aircraft type is learning the memory items for an emergency procedure. What is the type of learning described?

options.a: operative conditioning, which is a behaviouristic type of learning: recognising the physical stimuli (e.g. warning lights) associated with the emergency and learning to carry out the correct actions automatically.
options.b: classical conditioning, learning to react immediately to the physical stimuli (e.g. warning lights) associated with the emergency and activating the correct procedure automatically.
options.c: trial-and-error learning, where the pilot practises repeatedly in order to correctly complete the emergency procedure, without hesitation, from detecting the stimuli (e.g. warning lights) to a safe recovery.
options.d: cognitive learning, which involves understanding the emergency and its possible causes, knowledge of the aircraft, and integrating the information from the Manual with the pilot's aeronautical knowledge.
